english: Dzl., Med. sesame-seed; zar-mai me-tog (zar mai me tog) flower of sesame, Sch.; zar-ma-ču (zar ma chu) is mentioned in Pth. as Aphrodisiacum; yet zar-mai ras (zar mai ras) is stated to be a fabric, manufactured from zwa-tsód (zwa tshod), muslin?
Ld. a small and uninhabited river-island.
1. young cow, heifer, འbri-zál ('bri zal) yak-heifer. -- 2. a fabulous bird Sch.

food, nourishment, for men and animals, also in a wider fig. sense; zas-bčúd smyúṅ-gnas (zas bcud smyung gnas) fasting, abstaining from or withholding food Lex.; zas-bzáṅ(-po) (zas bzang (-po)) 1. dainty food Dzl. 2. nourishing fare, Wdn., zas-ṅán(-pa) (zas ngan (-pa)) the contrary; zas-ni (zas ni) as to diet ... Med.; zás-su či za (zas su ci za) what does it feed on? Dzl.; zás-su khrag འtúṅ-ba (zas su khrag 'thung ba) to drink blood for nourishment Do.; zas འtsól-ba (zas 'tshol ba) to seek to obtain a livelihood Ma.; འtsó-ba zas ('tsho ba zas), Mil. a pleon. expression = zas (zas); k̆a-zás (kha zas) (resp. žál-zas (zhal zas) B., sól-wa (gsol ba) col.) food, meat, for human beings; dkár-zas (dkar zas) v. dkár-ba (dkar ba); dmár-zas (dmar zas) Sch.: 'festival dishes', perh. more corr. flesh-meat, animal food? gró-zas (gro zas) Sch.: 'dry traveller's fare'; ṕán-zas (phan zas), wholesome nutritive food Med. — Comp. *ze-kóṅ* (*ze kong*) C. dearth, scarcity. — zas-skom (zas skom) meat and drink, solid and liquid food Med.; zas-čáṅ (zas chang), id., as travelling-provisions Glr. — zas-spyod (zas spyod) food and exercise, diet, in a wider sense Med. — zas-tsód (zas tshod) the due measure of food, zas-tsód ma zin (zas tshod ma zin) the portion or share was not full, it was not the full allowance, S.g. — zas-gtsáṅ-ma (zas gtsang ma) (clean food), n.p. शुद्धोदन, the father of Buddha; bdúd-rtsi-zas (bdud rtsi zas), bré-bo-zas (bre bo zas), zas-dkár (zas dkar) the names of his three brothers, zas-gtsaṅ-srás (zas gtsang sras) appellation of Buddha himself.

I. num. figure: 52. — II. W. 1. something of a very small size or quantity, *zi yaṅ mi dug* (*zi yang mi dug*) not an atom is left, *zi-med-kan čo* (*zi med kan co*) eat it up to the last crumb! *mé-zi* (*me zi*) a spark in the ashes ever so small. — 2. the black mark in a target. (cf. ža (zha)).
Sch.: green slime on standing water, zí-ma-čan (zi ma can) what is covered with such a slime.

Ssk. and Hindi जीर, the Asiatic caraway, Cuminum Cyminum, exported from Tibet to India, of a powerful aroma, which to the taste of Europeans is often disagreeable; two kinds are distinguished, zí-ra dkár-po (zi ra dkar po), and nág-po (nag po).
the humming of bees, the singing of a kettle W.

I. also *zi-ṅíl* (*zi ngil*), zí-lóṅ* (*zi long*) W. noise, bustle, tumult. — II. from the Chinese 1. also zi-lím (zi lim), zi-láṅ (zi lang) a composition metal, similar to German silver, zi-líṅ-pan-tse (zi ling pan tse) or baṅ-tse (bang tse) C. a basin of that metal. — 2. n. p., province, adjoining the Kokonor, zi-líṅ-ja (zi ling ja) tea from thence.
W., prob. for འdziṅ-sbrél ('dzing sbrel), with *čó-če* (*co ce*), to prepare for battle, or to begin fighting.

1. v. འdzín-pa ('dzin pa). — 2. = འdzád-pa ('dzad pa), esp. in the pf. tense, to draw near to an end, to be at an end, to be finished, exhausted, consumed; zin-pai ṕúṅ-po (zin pai phung po) the perishable, mortal body Thgy.; to be finished, terminated, nam yaṅ mi zin-to (nam yang mi zin to) Dzl. it will never be finished; to finish, to get done with, building a wall Glr., *zin čúg-če* (*zin cug ce*) W. id.; འtuṅ ma zin dógs-pas ('thung ma zin dogs pas) fearing not to be able to drink it all Glr.; rtsé-ba zin-pas (rtse ba zin pas) as the playing has ceased, or, as he has done playing Dzl.; zin(-pa) méd(-pai) las (zin (-pa) med (-pai) las) endless working, unceasing labour Mil.; hence = tsár-ba (tshar ba), to denote an action that is perfectly past, esp. in B., ṕrú-gu skyés-su zin kyaṅ (phru gu skyes su zin kyang) although the child is already born Do.; gsón-poi tsé-na འú-čag-gis de spyad zin (gson poi tshe na 'u cag gis de spyad zin) we had enjoyed it during our life-time; zin-brís (zin bris) Cs.: 1. abridgment, general view, synopsis. 2. lecture, so Schf. Tar. 210, 22. 3. receipt, quittance; bond (of obligation), bill of debt.
fine, thin, slender, čar zim-bu mi drág-po žig bab (char zim bu mi drag po zhig bab) a fine, drizzling
